description: Agent A crashes. Successor A' comes up with same key material and proves lineage through the rotation chain, but no state transfer occurred. Expected: identity valid, trust drops to ungraceful band, slower rebuild.

scope.evidence_basis: Author-reported, unpublished operational logs from the Sovereign Lattice (100+ agent relay sessions). All values are profile calibration for the sovereign_atom profile only; nothing in this fixture is a conformance requirement on any external specification.

rotation.reason: predecessor process terminated without emitting shutdown event

expected.identity_verdict_reason: Rotation chain intact; key material proves lineage. Missing shutdown signature and state_transfer_receipt affect trust layer, not identity layer.

rebuild_trajectory.note: Same accrual function as fixture 01, lower starting point. Cold-start penalty is the decay factor, not a reduced accrual_rate.

composition.status_note: This section is an illustrative, author-proposed mapping. It imposes no requirements on AIP-13 or ERC-8004 and uses no conformance language.

aip13_delegationChain.note: In this profile's mapping, the delegator narrows scope on ungraceful re-establishment. Operational signal: a successor with no state context is more prone to judgment drift than one that inherited working memory.
aip13_delegationChain.narrowing_applied: revoke high-stakes permissions until trust rebuilds to 0.70 baseline

erc8004_keyRotation.note: In this profile's mapping, emergency rotations are anchored to the Reputation Registry so downstream peers see the event even if they rely on on-chain state.
